1364.reason: The line number reported may be incorrect if curTokenIndex - 1 does not correspond to the last token of the preceding non-terminal, or if the token's stored line number is inaccurate due to lexer issues. Given the discrepancy (expected 3, got 2), the token at curTokenIndex - 1 likely has an incorrect line number assigned by the Lexer, but this is the reporting site.

reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 1.0 , reason:  The line number reported may be incorrect if curTokenIndex - 1 does not correspond to the last token of the preceding non-terminal, or if the token's stored line number is inaccurate due to lexer issues. Given the discrepancy (expected 3, got 2), the token at curTokenIndex - 1 likely has an incorrect line number assigned by the Lexer, but this is the reporting site.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 1.0 , reason:  [The error line number should correspond to the line where the opening parenthesis '(' was located, not the current token position. Using tokens.get(curTokenIndex - 1).getLineno() gives the line of the last parsed token, which may not be where the '(' appeared.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 1.0 , reason:  [The error line number should correspond to the line containing the opening parenthesis '(', not the previous token. According to the problem description, for error type 'j' (missing right parenthesis), the error line number should be the line where the last non-terminal before the right parenthesis is located, which should be tracked from when the '(' was parsed, not just using curTokenIndex - 1.]

194.reason: The newline character is consumed but curLine is not incremented, causing subsequent tokens to have incorrect line numbers.
